findings.title: subphase_closeouts source_micro_phase_id uses full subphase id rather than micro-phase id
findings.evidence: The 56 new subphase_closeouts entries set source_micro_phase_id to the FULL subphase id (e.g. "RP00.P07.M08.S01") instead of the micro-phase id (e.g. "RP00.P07.M08"). This diverges from the current_subphase convention in the same contract, which uses the micro-phase id form ("RP00.P08.M04"). The contract validator (scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs) passes, so this is non-blocking, but the inconsistency could confuse downstream tooling that joins on source_micro_phase_id or that assumes a single id granularity across ledger entries. I could not open the prior closed entries (CP00-083..CP00-087) to confirm which convention is canonical.
findings.recommended_action: Verify the source_micro_phase_id format used by the prior closed subphase_closeouts entries (CP00-083..CP00-087) and align the CP00-088 entries to the same convention. If full-subphase-id is in fact the established ledger convention and current_subphase is intentionally micro-phase-scoped, no change is needed—just confirm the divergence is intentional.

findings.title: Unit titles for RP00.P07.M08/M09/M10 reuse failure-taxonomy labels that may not match micro-phase scope
findings.evidence: In CONTROL_PLANE_FAILURE_EDGE_RECOVERY_EVIDENCE_REVIEW_HANDOFF_UNITS, the unit titles for micro-phases RP00.P07.M08/M09/M10 reuse failure-taxonomy labels (e.g. "Failure taxonomy", "Missing tenant failure", "Missing actor failure") carried over from the upstream test-golden-case-set pack, whereas these micro-phases are scoped to Hermes Evidence Packet / Claude Review Packet / Closeout And Next Handoff. The titles are descriptive metadata only and do not affect boundary enforcement or validation (which key off ids and field-sets), so impact is cosmetic, but mismatched labels reduce ledger readability.
findings.recommended_action: Confirm the intended per-unit titles against docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json and update the title/microTitle strings to match the actual micro-phase scope if they were inadvertently copied from the upstream pack. No behavioral change required.

findings.title: RP00.P08.M00-M04 marked production_ready/completed while described as being 'opened'
findings.evidence: The pack purpose describes opening Hermes Validation Binding through RP00.P08.M04 as metadata-only evidence contracts, yet the corresponding subphase_closeouts entries are marked status=production_ready / completed. This matches the project's established contract-first semantics (a subphase is 'production_ready' once its evidence contract is bound, not once runtime code exists) and is consistent with all runtime/write flags being false, so it is not a runtime overclaim. Flagging only because the 'opened' wording vs. 'production_ready' status could be misread by a reviewer unfamiliar with the metadata-only ledger model.
findings.recommended_action: Confirm the ledger semantics are intended (evidence-bound = production_ready) and, if helpful for future readers, ensure the README/contract wording makes explicit that 'opening' a micro-phase means binding its metadata-only evidence contract, not shipping runtime behavior. No change required for closeout.

parsed_review.raw_review_excerpt: CP00-088 review submitted: **PASS_WITH_FINDINGS**, `blocks_closeout: false`. Three non-blocking findings (one P2 ledger-convention divergence on `source_micro_phase_id`, two P3 cosmetic/semantic items); no P0/P1 correctness, boundary, or overclaim issues — closeout is clear to proceed.
parsed_review.summary: Read-only Opus 4.8 max review of Closeout Pack CP00-088 (Risk C, 56 units, RP00.P07.M08.S01–RP00.P08.M04.S11; close Failure Edge Recovery terminal Hermes evidence/Claude review/closeout handoff metadata and open Hermes Validation Binding through RP00.P08.M04 as metadata-only evidence contracts). VERDICT: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS, non-blocking. The pack is consistent with the established contract-first / metadata-only pattern and the boundary is airtight. Internal consistency checks pass: unit count 56 (11+11+3+1+4+4+11+11) across 8 source micro-phases; hermes_validation_receipt_matrix=31 (P08 M00=1, M01=4, M02=4, M03=11, M04=11); hermes_validation_opening_contracts=5; next_subphase RP00.P08.M05.S01 aligns with open_source_micro_phase_ids and the closeout-handoff next_pack_subphase_id; current_subphase=RP00.P08.M04.S11. No-write/no-runtime boundary is enforced: all runtime/write/audit/permission/Claude-invocation/LDIP flags are false, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false; the assembler fails closed on any truthy boundary claim (forbidden→"cannot claim X", unknown→"cannot accept unknown claim X") and on upstream drift (requires upstream pack_id===CP00-087 && planned_pack_id===CP00-087 and next_subphase==="RP00.P07.M08.S01"); the result validator enforces exact key-set equality plus scalar/array/contract deep-equality against policy. CP00-087→CP00-088 chaining is enforced. The Claude review packet contract declares claude-opus-4-8 / max / read-only with runtime_claude_review_invocation_permitted=false; LDIP remains planning-only with no implementation drift observed. production_ready reflects the project's established "evidence bound" semantics, not a runtime overclaim. No P0/P1 issues found. IMPORTANT LIMITATION: local file-system tools were unavailable in this session, so this review is based on the supplied unified diff plus the provided Codex pre-validation results (node --check all pass; targeted node --test 6 pass; service.test.js 805 pass; npm test 889 pass; validate/spec/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/build all pass). Findings 01 and 02 are verification items I could not confirm against the plan file or prior closed entries; none block closeout.
